# ADR-001: Target netstandard2.0

<!-- Generated by seed-golden-context | Last updated: 2026-05-11 -->

## Status

Accepted

## Context

The SDK needs to run on any .NET runtime where customers build Contentful-backed applications: .NET Framework 4.6.1+, .NET Core 2.0+, Mono, Xamarin, and later .NET 5/6/7/8. Customers using older .NET Framework versions are common in enterprise .NET shops.

Targeting a specific runtime (e.g., `net6.0`) would exclude a large portion of the .NET ecosystem. The library has no OS-native dependencies or runtime-specific APIs that would require a higher target.

## Decision

Both `Contentful.Core` and `Contentful.AspNetCore` target `netstandard2.0`. This is the broadest compatibility profile that still provides a rich BCL (including `HttpClient`, `Task`-based async, and the full collection types the SDK needs).

The SDK is described in official Contentful docs as "cross-platform, runs on Linux, macOS and Windows."

## Consequences

- **Enables:** Maximum consumer reach — any .NET runtime that implements netstandard2.0 can use this SDK.
- **Prevents:** Using APIs only available in newer runtimes (e.g., `System.Text.Json` built-ins, `HttpClient` improvements in .NET 5+) without either a multi-target setup or a breaking version bump.
- **Follow-up:** If a future version wants to leverage newer BCL features, consider adding a `net6.0` or `net8.0` target alongside `netstandard2.0` (multi-targeting) rather than dropping the standard target.
